name: DIFFUSE IDIOPATHIC SKELETAL HYPEROSTOSIS
definition: DISH = FORESTIER DISEASE = ANKYLOSING HYPEROSTOSIS common ossifying diathesis characterized by bone proliferation at sites of tendinous +

demographics.Location: middle + lower thoracic > lower cervical > entire lumbar spine

dominant_finding: increased incidence of hyperostosis frontalis interna
distribution: middle + lower thoracic > lower cervical > entire lumbar spine
discriminating_features: increased incidence of hyperostosis frontalis interna
flowing ossification of at least 4 contiguous vertebral bodies:
osteophytes located anteriorly + laterally on right side (not on left because of aortic
osteophytes largest at level of intervertebral disk

general: increased incidence of hyperostosis frontalis interna
Spine: flowing ossification of at least 4 contiguous vertebral bodies:
osteophytes located anteriorly + laterally on right side (not on left because of aortic
osteophytes largest at level of intervertebral disk
disk spaces well preserved, no apophyseal ankylosis, no sacroiliitis
Pelvis: bridge across superior aspect of symphysis pubis
ossification of iliolumbar + sacrotuberous + sacroiliac ligaments (high probability for
"whiskering" at iliac crest, ischial tuberosity, trochanters
broad osteophytes at lateral acetabular edge, inferior portions of sacroiliac joints
Extremities: big heel spurs (on plantar + posterior surface of calcaneus)
spur of olecranon process of ulna
spur on anterior surface of patella
ossification of coracoclavicular ligament, patellar ligament, tibial tuberosity, interosseous
